# Section 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics

> [!NOTE]
> **Diagram Note:** Transistor-level schematics are best drawn in the EDA tool schematic editor. Mermaid is used here to render the **block-level functional architecture** and **operational flow** of the 6T SRAM cell, the 1T1C DRAM cell, and the simulation workflow expected for the KTU 2024 project.

## 4.1 6T SRAM Cell — Functional Block Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph INV1["INVERTER I1 - TOP LEFT"]
        M1["M1 - PMOS PULL UP"]
        M2["M2 - NMOS PULL DOWN"]
    end
    subgraph INV2["INVERTER I2 - BOTTOM RIGHT"]
        M3["M3 - PMOS PULL UP"]
        M4["M4 - NMOS PULL DOWN"]
    end
    subgraph ACC["ACCESS TRANSISTORS - GATED BY WL"]
        M5["M5 - NMOS LEFT ACCESS"]
        M6["M6 - NMOS RIGHT ACCESS"]
    end
    Q["NODE Q - INTERNAL STORAGE LEFT"]
    QB["NODE QB - INTERNAL STORAGE RIGHT"]
    BL["BIT LINE BL"]
    BLB["BIT LINE BLB"]
    WL["WORD LINE WL"]
    VDD["VDD POWER RAIL"]
    GND["GROUND RAIL"]
    M1 --- Q
    M2 --- Q
    M3 --- QB
    M4 --- QB
    Q --- QB
    M5 --- Q
    M5 --- BL
    M6 --- QB
    M6 --- BLB
    M5 --- WL
    M6 --- WL
    M1 --- VDD
    M3 --- VDD
    M2 --- GND
    M4 --- GND
```

## 4.2 6T SRAM — Read Operation Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A["STEP 1 - PRECHARGE BL AND BLB TO VDD"] --> B["STEP 2 - ASSERT WL TO VDD"]
    B --> C["ACCESS TRANSISTORS M5 M6 TURN ON"]
    C --> D["NODE HOLDING ZERO DISCHARGES BL THROUGH PULL DOWN NMOS"]
    D --> E["SENSE AMPLIFIER DETECTS DELTA V BETWEEN BL AND BLB"]
    E --> F{"IS DELTA V GREATER THAN SENSE AMP OFFSET"}
    F -- YES --> G["OUTPUT VALID READ DATA"]
    F -- NO --> H["READ FAILURE - INCREASE CR"]
```

## 4.3 6T SRAM — Write Operation Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A["STEP 1 - DRIVE BL WITH NEW DATA - ZERO TARGET"] --> B["STEP 2 - DRIVE BLB TO VDD"]
    B --> C["STEP 3 - ASSERT WL TO VDD"]
    C --> D["ACCESS TRANSISTOR M5 PULLS Q TOWARDS ZERO"]
    D --> E{"Q BELOW TRIP POINT OF INVERTER I2"}
    E -- YES --> F["CELL FLIPS - WRITE SUCCESS"]
    E -- NO --> G["WRITE FAILURE - REDUCE PR OR INCREASE WL DRIVE"]
```

## 4.4 1T1C DRAM Cell — Block Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    WL["WORD LINE WL"]
    M1["M1 - NMOS ACCESS TRANSISTOR"]
    CS["CS - STORAGE CAPACITOR - TYP 25 FF"]
    BL["BIT LINE BL"]
    SA["SENSE AMPLIFIER"]
    REF["DUMMY CELL REFERENCE"]
    WL --- M1
    M1 --- CS
    M1 --- BL
    BL --- SA
    REF --- SA
    SA --> OUT["DIGITAL OUTPUT - 0 OR 1"]
```

## 4.5 DRAM Read — Charge Sharing Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A["STEP 1 - PRECHARGE BL TO VDD DIVIDED BY 2"] --> B["STEP 2 - ASSERT WL"]
    B --> C["CHARGE SHARING BETWEEN CS AND CBL"]
    C --> D["BL VOLTAGE SHIFTS BY DELTA VBL APPROX 100 MV"]
    D --> E["REFERENCE CELL PROVIDES OPPOSITE SHIFT"]
    E --> F["SENSE AMPLIFIER LATCHES DIFFERENTIAL"]
    F --> G["OUTPUT VALID READ DATA"]
```

## 4.6 KTU 2024 Project — End-to-End Simulation Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    P1["PHASE 1 - SPECIFICATION - DEFINE VDD NODE TARGET SNM AND AREA"] --> P2["PHASE 2 - SCHEMATIC - DRAW 6T SRAM AND 1T1C DRAM CELLS IN EDA TOOL"]
    P2 --> P3["PHASE 3 - TRANSISTOR SIZING - APPLY CR GREATER THAN 1.2 AND PR LESS THAN 1.5"]
    P3 --> P4["PHASE 4 - NETLIST GENERATION - HSPICE OR NGSPICE FORMAT"]
    P4 --> P5["PHASE 5 - DC ANALYSIS - SWEEP WL AND BL TO OBTAIN BUTTERFLY CURVE"]
    P5 --> P6["PHASE 6 - TRANSIENT ANALYSIS - VERIFY READ AND WRITE PULSE TIMING"]
    P6 --> P7["PHASE 7 - NOISE MARGIN - MEASURE SNM HOLD AND READ"]
    P7 --> P8["PHASE 8 - POWER AND DELAY - INTEGRATE CURRENT AND TIME WAVE FORMS"]
    P8 --> P9["PHASE 9 - REPORT - DOCUMENT VTCS SNM ACCESS TIME AND POWER"]
```

## 4.7 SNM Butterfly — Conceptual Plot

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph PLOT["BUTTERFLY CURVE WITH EMBEDDED 45 DEGREE LINES"]
        A1["POINT A - UPPER LEFT STABLE STATE 1"]
        A2["POINT B - LOWER RIGHT STABLE STATE 0"]
        A3["LOWER VTC OF INVERTER I1"]
        A4["UPPER MIRROR VTC OF INVERTER I2"]
        A5["SQUARE OF SIDE SNM INSCRIBED BETWEEN CURVES"]
        A1 --- A5
        A2 --- A5
        A3 --- A5
        A4 --- A5
    end
```

> [!TIP]
> **Reading the Butterfly Plot in the Exam:** A larger inscribed square means a *larger* noise tolerance. Cell designers maximise the inscribed square by increasing **CR** (push lower VTC up) and reducing **PR** (push upper VTC down).

# Section 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short-Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. [KTU University Exam – May 2024 Style]
**Differentiate between 6T SRAM and 1T1C DRAM cells with respect to cell structure, refresh requirement, area, speed, and power.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

| Aspect | 6T SRAM | 1T1C DRAM |
|---|---|---|
| Cell structure | 6 transistors (2 cross-coupled inverters + 2 access) | 1 access transistor + 1 storage capacitor |
| Area | ~120–150 F² (large) | ~6–8 F² (very dense) |
| Refresh | Not required (static) | Required every ~64 ms (dynamic) |
| Speed | Faster (ns access) | Slower (sense amp settling, ~10 ns) |
| Active power | Lower (no refresh) | Refresh power dominates |
| Standby power | Higher (DC leakage paths) | Lower (only sub-threshold leak) |
| Use | CPU cache, registers | Main memory |

(1 mark per major difference, condensed within 3-mark limit)

### Q2. [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023 Style]
**Define Static Noise Margin (SNM) of a 6T SRAM cell. State the typical read-SNM and hold-SNM target values for a 180 nm cell with $V_{DD}$ = 1.8 V.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- **Definition (2 marks):** SNM is the maximum DC noise voltage that can be tolerated at the storage nodes of a bistable SRAM cell without causing a destructive read or a data flip. It is computed graphically as the **side length of the largest square that can be inscribed** between the lower VTC of inverter I1 and the mirrored upper VTC of inverter I2, bounded by the two 45° lines $V_{out} = V_{in}$ and $V_{out} = V_{in} - V_n$.
- **Targets (1 mark):** For a 180 nm cell with $V_{DD} = 1.8$ V, **Read SNM ≥ 0.4 × 1.8 = 0.72 V** and **Hold SNM ≥ 0.2 × 1.8 = 0.36 V**.

## 5.2 Part B — Module Internal Choice (14 Marks Each)

### Question A (14 Marks) [KTU University Exam – Model Paper Style]

**(a) [7 Marks]** Design a 6T SRAM cell in 180 nm CMOS with $V_{DD}$ = 1.8 V. Specify transistor sizes (W/L) for M1–M6, the word-line pulse width, and the bit-line pre-charge voltage. Justify your sizing using the cell-ratio (CR) and pull-up ratio (PR) inequalities.

**(b) [7 Marks]** Explain, with sketches, the *butterfly curve method* of computing the read SNM. If the simulated SNM is found to be 0.30 V for a $V_{DD}$ = 1.0 V 90 nm cell, comment on whether the cell is production-ready. Suggest two design modifications to improve the SNM.

---

**Model Solution:**

**Part (a) — Design Sizing (7 marks)**

**Step 1 — Technology and constraints (1 mark):** Use 180 nm CMOS, $V_{DD} = 1.8$ V, $V_{THN} = 0.45$ V, $V_{THP} = -0.50$ V, $L_{min} = 0.18$ µm. Use minimum-length devices for area efficiency, then adjust widths.

**Step 2 — Choose access transistor width (1 mark):** Use minimum size, $W/L = 0.24/0.18$ µm (a common 180 nm choice). So $(W/L)_{AX} = 1.33$.

**Step 3 — Apply CR ≥ 1.2 (2 marks):** $\text{CR} = (W/L)_{PD}/(W/L)_{AX} \geq 1.2$ ⇒ $(W/L)_{PD} \geq 1.6$. Choose $(W/L)_{PD} = (W/L)_{M2} = (W/L)_{M4} = 0.30/0.18 = 1.67$. CR = 1.67/1.33 = **1.25 ✓**

**Step 4 — Apply PR ≤ 1.5 (2 marks):** $\text{PR} = (W/L)_{PU}/(W/L)_{AX} \leq 1.5$ ⇒ $(W/L)_{PU} \leq 2.0$. Choose $(W/L)_{PU} = (W/L)_{M1} = (W/L)_{M3} = 0.30/0.18 = 1.67$. PR = 1.67/1.33 = **1.25 ✓**

**Step 5 — Final cell spec (1 mark):**
- M1, M3 (PMOS pull-up): W/L = 0.30/0.18 µm
- M2, M4 (NMOS pull-down): W/L = 0.30/0.18 µm
- M5, M6 (NMOS access): W/L = 0.24/0.18 µm
- Word-line pulse: 1.0 ns, 0→1.8 V square pulse
- Pre-charge BL/BLB to $V_{DD} = 1.8$ V

**Part (b) — SNM Butterfly Method (7 marks)**

**Step 1 — Method (3 marks):** Build the **VTC of inverter I1** (input `Q`, output `QB`) using DC sweep, then plot its **mirror image** as the VTC of inverter I2. The two curves form the butterfly shape. Embed two 45° lines: $V_{out} = V_{in}$ and $V_{out} = V_{in} - V_n$. The side of the largest inscribed square is the **SNM**.

**Step 2 — Quantitative assessment (2 marks):** Reported SNM = 0.30 V at $V_{DD} = 1.0$ V. Ratio = 0.30/1.0 = **0.30**. This is **below the 0.40 target**, so the cell is **NOT production-ready**; it is vulnerable to read disturb at process/voltage/temperature corners.

**Step 3 — Two design fixes (2 marks):**
1. **Increase the cell ratio CR** by widening the pull-down NMOS (e.g., raise $W_2$ by 20%) — this raises the lower VTC and increases the inscribed square.
2. **Lower the word-line voltage $V_{WL}$** below $V_{DD}$ (a *dynamic read-assist* technique called "WL underdrive") — this weakens the access transistor relative to the pull-down, increasing read stability at the cost of a slight speed penalty.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall:** Many students describe the butterfly method but **fail to draw or describe the two 45° lines** that bound the inscribed square. Without those lines, the SNM is undefined. Always include both 45° lines in any SNM diagram.

---

### Question B (14 Marks) [Alternative Internal Choice]

**(a) [7 Marks]** With a neat schematic, explain the operation of a **1T1C DRAM cell** during write, read, and refresh phases. Derive the bit-line voltage swing $\Delta V_{BL}$ when the cell capacitor is fully charged and the bit-line is pre-charged to $V_{DD}/2$. Use $C_S$ = 30 fF, $C_{BL}$ = 200 fF, $V_{DD}$ = 1.5 V.

**(b) [7 Marks]** Discuss the design challenges faced by modern DRAM as it scales below 20 nm, including leakage, refresh overhead, capacitance reduction, and sense-amp offset. Compare briefly with the scaling challenges of 6T SRAM.

---

**Model Solution:**

**Part (a) — 1T1C DRAM Operation and $\Delta V_{BL}$ (7 marks)**

**Step 1 — Schematic description (1 mark):** 1 NMOS access transistor (M1) connects storage capacitor $C_S$ to bit-line `BL`. Word-line `WL` is the gate of M1. A sense amplifier at the column end amplifies the small bit-line deviation.

**Step 2 — Write phase (1 mark):** Drive `BL` to $V_{DD}$ (or 0 V), assert `WL = V_{DD}$. M1 turns on and charges $C_S$ to $V_{BL}$ (minus threshold drop on a 0 V write).

**Step 3 — Hold phase (1 mark):** `WL = 0`, M1 is OFF. $C_S$ holds charge, but sub-threshold and junction leakage slowly discharge it.

**Step 4 — Read phase (1 mark):** Pre-charge `BL` to $V_{DD}/2$. Assert `WL`. Charge sharing between $C_S$ and $C_{BL}$ perturbs `BL` by a small amount; sense amp detects.

**Step 5 — Refresh phase (1 mark):** Read the cell, then immediately rewrite the sensed value back to $C_S$ to restore the lost charge.

**Step 6 — Derive $\Delta V_{BL}$ (2 marks):** From Section 3.4:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\Delta V_{BL} &= \frac{C_S}{C_S + C_{BL}}\cdot\frac{V_{DD}}{2} \\[4pt]
&= \frac{30 \text{ fF}}{30 \text{ fF} + 200 \text{ fF}}\cdot\frac{1.5 \text{ V}}{2} \\[4pt]
&= \frac{30}{230}\cdot 0.75 = 0.1304\cdot 0.75 \approx 0.098 \text{ V} \approx 98 \text{ mV}
\end{aligned}
$$

**Part (b) — DRAM Scaling Challenges (7 marks)**

**Challenge 1 — Capacitance reduction (2 marks):** As feature size shrinks, $C_S$ falls (cell area shrinks). A 25 fF cell at 90 nm becomes ~5 fF at 20 nm, dropping $\Delta V_{BL}$ to ~25 mV. Tall trench or stacked capacitors are required to maintain capacitance.

**Challenge 2 — Leakage and refresh overhead (2 marks):** Sub-threshold and gate-tunnelling leakages grow exponentially with scaling. $t_{RET} = C_S \Delta V / I_{LEAK}$ shrinks; the array must be refreshed more often (e.g., 32 ms instead of 64 ms), consuming ~30% of total DRAM power.

**Challenge 3 — Sense-amp offset (1 mark):** As $\Delta V_{BL}$ falls, the random $V_{TH}$ mismatch of the sense-amp input pair becomes comparable. SRAM does not face this because its differential is rail-to-rail.

**Comparison with SRAM (2 marks):** SRAM scaling is *less* hindered by leakage in the storage element (no capacitor) but is *more* hindered by the read/write ratio trade-off (CR vs PR). 6T cells at 7 nm require **finFETs** and aggressive read/write assist circuits. DRAM scaling uses **3D stacked cells (HBM, HBM2E, HBM3)** rather than finFETs.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall (Part b):** Students often write only the *DRAM* challenges and forget the explicit *comparison with SRAM*. A 7-mark question expects a balanced two-sided discussion. Always include at least one comparative sentence.

## 5.3 KTU 2024 Project Viva Voce — Likely Questions (2–4 Marks Each)

| # | Likely Question | Expected Key Words in Answer |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Why is the SRAM cell called "6T"? | Six transistors: 2 PMOS pull-ups, 2 NMOS pull-downs, 2 NMOS access |
| 2 | What is the role of the cell ratio? | Read stability; CR ≥ 1.2 prevents read disturb |
| 3 | What limits DRAM density? | Minimum $C_S$ needed for adequate $\Delta V_{BL}$ and retention |
| 4 | Why does DRAM need refresh? | Capacitor leakage; data lost in milliseconds |
| 5 | What is the typical refresh interval? | 64 ms (JEDEC standard for DDR4) |
| 6 | Name a circuit technique to improve read SNM. | WL underdrive, dynamic read-assist, 8T/10T cell |
| 7 | What is DRV? | Data Retention Voltage; minimum $V_{DD}$ for hold stability |
| 8 | Why use minimum-length access transistors? | Area and bit-line load reduction |

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Rapid-Revision Checklist (Read this 5 minutes before the exam):**

- **6T SRAM cell structure:** 2 cross-coupled CMOS inverters (M1–M4) + 2 access NMOS (M5–M6).
- **Standard sizing rule (180 nm):** CR = (W/L)_PD / (W/L)_AX ≥ **1.2**; PR = (W/L)_PU / (W/L)_AX ≤ **1.5**.
- **Read SNM target:** ≥ **0.4 × $V_{DD}$**; **Hold SNM target:** ≥ **0.2 × $V_{DD}$**.
- **SNM measurement method:** Largest inscribed square in the **butterfly curve** of two mirrored VTCs, bounded by 45° lines.
- **DRAM cell structure:** 1T1C — one access NMOS + one storage capacitor (~$C_S = 25$–30 fF at 180 nm).
- **DRAM read swing:** $\Delta V_{BL} = \dfrac{C_S}{C_S + C_{BL}} \cdot \dfrac{V_{DD}}{2}$ (typically 100–250 mV).
- **DRAM retention time:** $t_{RET} = \dfrac{C_S \Delta V}{I_{LEAK}}$ (must exceed **64 ms**).
- **DRAM refresh:** Read and rewrite every cell periodically to compensate leakage.
- **Area benchmark:** SRAM ≈ 120–150 F²; DRAM ≈ 6–8 F² ⇒ DRAM is **~20× denser**.
- **Speed benchmark:** SRAM access ≈ 1–2 ns; DRAM access ≈ 10 ns.
- **Power benchmark:** SRAM has higher active power per cell; DRAM has higher idle power due to refresh.
- **Project deliverable checklist:** (1) Schematic in EDA tool, (2) sized netlist with W/L values, (3) DC sweep showing butterfly curve, (4) transient waveforms for read/write, (5) tabulated SNM, $t_A$, and power, (6) short report explaining design choices and trade-offs.
- **Common KTU 2024 mistakes to avoid:** Forgetting the 45° lines in the SNM plot, quoting CR/PR inequalities backward, confusing read and hold SNM targets, omitting the comparison with SRAM in DRAM questions.

> [!TIP]
> **Final Advice:** In the 14-mark Part-B question, the KTU examiner's model answer typically awards 2 marks for a labelled diagram, 3 marks for the analytical step, and 2 marks for the design justification/calculation. Always budget time accordingly: draw first, derive second, justify third.
